questions.question: Which statement best explains why K-Means uses squared Euclidean distance instead of simple Euclidean distance?

answers.b: To penalize larger deviations more strongly

explanations.b: Squared distance gives more weight to points far from centroid, improving cluster compactness.
explanations.c: K-Means does not normalize data automatically.
explanations.d: Squaring does not change the spherical assumption.

questions.question: What is the main reason K-Means may produce different results on different runs?

explanations.a: Initial centroid selection is random, leading to different final clusters.
explanations.b: Hardware does not affect clustering results.
explanations.c: Dataset size remains constant in same run.
explanations.d: K-Means is not fully deterministic due to initialization.

questions.question: Which condition ensures that K-Means has converged?

explanations.a: Convergence occurs when centroid positions no longer change.
explanations.b: This is a stopping condition but not true convergence.
explanations.d: Equal cluster size is not required.

questions.question: The distance of a point in a cluster to another point in the same cluster is generally

answers.a: Greater than its own cluster points
answers.b: Lesser than its own cluster points

explanations.a: Points in different clusters usually have larger distances.
explanations.b: Points within the same cluster tend to be closer to each other.
explanations.d: Since points are usually closer within the cluster, this is incorrect.

questions.question: Why is feature scaling important in K-Means?

answers.b: To equalize influence of all features in distance calculation

explanations.a: Scaling does not affect K directly.
explanations.b: Features with large scale dominate distance if not normalized.
explanations.c: It helps but does not guarantee accuracy.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the role of centroids?

explanations.b: Centroids are mean positions of clusters.
explanations.c: Data is not stored in centroids.
explanations.d: Noise removal is not their function.

questions.question: If clusters are highly overlapped, this indicates

explanations.a: Overlapping clusters mean that the separation between groups is weak.
explanations.b: Good clustering should have clearly separated groups.
explanations.c: Overlap indicates low clustering quality rather than average quality.
explanations.d: Since overlapping means poor clustering, this option is incorrect.

questions.question: Why does K-Means prefer compact clusters?

answers.a: Because it minimizes variance within clusters

explanations.d: Cluster size is not maximized explicitly.

questions.question: Which property of K-Means makes it unsuitable for categorical data?

explanations.b: Mean cannot be computed for categorical features.

questions.question: Why is K-Means not suitable for clusters with complex shapes

answers.a: Because it assumes clusters are spherical around centroids
answers.d: Because it cannot handle numerical data

explanations.a: K-Means assumes clusters are roughly spherical and evenly sized.
explanations.b: Distance computation is the core of K-Means.
explanations.d: K-Means primarily works with numerical data.
